page: Google Cloud Platform t2a-standard-16 is an ARM-based general-purpose server instance powered by the t2a family. It features 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs running on the ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 architecture at a clock speed of 3.0 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Cache specifications consist of 64 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache. This instance does not include local storage or integrated GPU hardware.

Performance benchmarks position this instance primarily in the average tier for multi-core and memory operations. The stress-ng multi-core CPU test yields a score of 28983.31, while the single-core performance registers at 1813.28. Memory bandwidth tests show 1129.87 GB/sec for cached small block reads and 129.41 GB/sec for uncached large block reads. Single-threaded tasks like bzip3 decompression and Geekbench single-core evaluations show weaker relative performance, scoring 8.61 GB/sec and 1078.0 respectively. However, multi-threaded tasks such as software compilation with Clang and ray tracing perform within the average range.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated ARM architecture offers a balanced resource density for scale-out workloads without the overhead of hyperthreading. Tradeoffs include weaker single-threaded performance and lower cryptographic execution speeds, as evidenced by a weak Passmark CPU encryption score of 2588.14. The instance is suited for workloads such as static web serving, where it achieves a throughput of 18.26 GB/sec, database operations, and Redis deployments, which average 21.18 million operations per second for SET commands.
description: Google Cloud Platform t2a-standard-16 is an ARM-based general-purpose server instance featuring 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs on the ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 architecture. Operating at 3.0 GHz, the system is configured with 64.0 GB of RAM, providing 4.0 GB of memory per core, with no bundled local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmark data indicates average multi-core and memory bandwidth performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 28983.31 and a static web serving throughput of 18.26 GB/sec. Single-threaded operations and cryptographic tasks present performance tradeoffs, scoring in the weaker percentile. This instance is suited for scale-out workloads, database operations, Redis key-value stores, and static web serving.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform t2a-standard-16 is an ARM-based server with 16 dedicated Neoverse-N1 cores, 64 GB RAM, and average multi-core performance suited for web serving and database workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform t2a-standard-16 is an ARM-based instance with 16 vCPUs and 64 GB RAM, optimized for balanced general-purpose workloads.
tagline: An ARM-powered general-purpose instance delivering dedicated physical cores and balanced memory allocation for scale-out workloads and web serving.

64.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core
Average multi-core performance with 28983.31 stress-ng score
